judgment.summary: Workday offers high growth and strong cash flow at a highly attractive forward valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS of 2.47 beat the 2.32 estimate by 6.4%, with the next earnings date set for 2026-05-21.
positives: PEG ratio 0.46 — indicates the stock is undervalued relative to its growth profile.
Forward P/E 9.89 — suggests a much more attractive valuation than the trailing 47.54x.
Earnings growth 62.9% — shows significant year-over-year momentum.
Free cash flow $3.01B — demonstrates strong cash generation.
Gross margin 75.7% — reflects high-quality core product profitability.
negatives: Profit margin 7.3% — indicates relatively thin net income conversion.
Operating margin 12.0% — shows moderate efficiency in managing operating expenses.
Return on equity 8.2% — reflects modest returns on shareholder capital.

news.paragraph: Workday has recently been named a leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for Talent Acquisition suites and is enhancing its enterprise AI capabilities through new Microsoft 365 Copilot integrations. These product developments and the potential strengthening of its AI moat provide a positive news backdrop for the company. Options traders should consider these technological advancements alongside broader market trends and recent analyst research calls.
